persons.arguments_against: Strongest counter-case: the bio carries only initials ('J. W.'), and the corpus has a second Bonser in the staff lists, so the stint could in principle belong to that other Bonser — perhaps a relative who also reached the bench. This fails decisively. Chief Justice of Ceylon is a unique office held by one person at a time; the bio says 'ch. justice, Ceylon, 1893' and the staff list shows J. W. Bonser as Chief Justice continuously 1894-1900, the exact years the bio holder occupied the post. The stint's printed style 'Sir J. W. Bonser' from 1894 matches the bio's 'KT. BACH. (1894)' knighthood date precisely — an independent corroborating dimension beyond name and office. A second J. W. Bonser cannot have been Chief Justice of Ceylon in the same years. The single-initial caution does not apply because the link rests on a specific unique-office match with date and honour-style agreement, not on tenure overlap alone.
persons.would_overturn: Documentation that a different person was Chief Justice of Ceylon in 1894-1900, or evidence that the staff-list rows conflate two Bonsers (e.g. a second J. W. Bonser appearing simultaneously in the Ceylon judicial establishment).

notes: Stint ends by 1900, consistent with the bio's elevation to the Judicial Committee of the Privy Council in 1901 ('mem. jud. comt. depute, priv. coun., 1901').
